My my application that I deployed for the company currently being used by 300 people and potentially by thousand users across the globe is found a very like we. I felt a very major issue when it scaled. that is that we were using Snoop like bass snowflake as the back end and we were getting IP addresses restricted because and each and every IPR to be white listed because it was hosted on AWS amplify now, unlike worcel amplify still has not been able to give a white listing egress IP address right? so it does not have that feature. so because of this there was a lot of user problems. so the app would kind of feel clunky, glitchy errored etc. what I was told by an amazing colleague. is that why not? let's all shift to workforce identity federation so that AWS are identified by snowflake and a specific role is created for my app. now this change the game overnight. I had to pick up all the AWS infrastructure that I had created all the lambda functions all the all of the things which are required which have a connection to Snoop lake and shifted them from using JWT tokens to snowflake into using workforce identity federation and had to scare configure users, configure roles and give extreme accesses and then I created test branch and have deployed it in a test. now I'm monitoring it. it has no IP problems till now and I think this is a little pivot point in our application which will make the users journey. UI ux feels so much more better and I just have this different rash of dopamine by doing it. this was one of the biggest challenges of the app and I think it was fixed by just workflows identity federation. it is one of the best things I feel I found
